title: Add basic traffic violation detection with liability percentages

requirements.text: Extend the backend AccidentReport type to include a violations array that captures detected traffic violations (stop sign violations, U-turn violations, traffic signal violations) with fields for violation type, description, and detected timestamp

quotes: Let's focus on stop signs violations, U-turn, signal lights etc.

acceptanceCriteria: AccidentReport type includes a violations field as an array of violation records
Each violation record includes type (stop sign, U-turn, traffic signal), description, and timestamp

requirements.text: Add liability assessment fields to the backend AccidentReport type to store fault percentage splits between parties (e.g., party1Liability: 70%, party2Liability: 30%)

quotes: I think we should include liability at this stay, example 70% 30%

acceptanceCriteria: AccidentReport type includes liability percentage fields for each party
Liability percentages are stored as Natural numbers representing percentages
The sum of all liability percentages equals 100

requirements.text: Implement backend logic to detect stop sign violations by analyzing whether a stop sign was present in detected traffic signs and if the vehicle velocity indicates the driver did not come to a complete stop

quotes: Let's focus on stop signs violations

acceptanceCriteria: Logic checks for stop sign presence in trafficSigns array
Logic evaluates velocity data to determine if vehicle stopped
Violation is recorded when stop sign detected and velocity indicates no stop

requirements.text: Implement backend logic to detect U-turn violations by accepting GPS location data and checking if a U-turn was performed in a prohibited area based on detected 'No U-turn' traffic signs

quotes: It can accept Gps location and timestamp information

acceptanceCriteria: Backend accepts GPS location data in the report submission
Logic checks for 'No U-turn' signs in detected traffic signs
Violation is recorded when no U-turn sign is present and location data suggests illegal U-turn

requirements.text: Implement backend logic to detect traffic signal violations by comparing the witness testimony traffic light color against the AI-detected signal state and flagging discrepancies as potential violations
acceptanceCriteria: Logic compares trafficSignalColor testimony with detected signal state
Red light violation is flagged when mismatch indicates driver ran red light
Traffic signal violation is added to violations array

requirements.text: Create a backend function that calculates liability percentages based on detected violations, assigning higher fault percentage to the party with more serious or multiple violations

quotes: I think we should include liability at this stay, example 70% 30%

acceptanceCriteria: Function analyzes all detected violations for each party
Function returns percentage split totaling 100%
More violations or serious violations result in higher liability percentage
Liability percentages are stored in the AccidentReport

requirements.text: Update the AccidentReportForm component to accept optional GPS location coordinates (latitude, longitude) and timestamp for enhanced violation detection

quotes: It can accept Gps location and timestamp information

acceptanceCriteria: Form includes optional GPS location input fields
Form includes timestamp field (defaults to current time)
GPS and timestamp data are submitted with the accident report

requirements.text: Create a ViolationsDisplay component that shows all detected violations in a list format with violation type, description, and timestamp for each violation

quotes: Let's focus on stop signs violations, U-turn, signal lights etc.

acceptanceCriteria: Component displays violations array from AccidentReport
Each violation shows type, description, and timestamp
Component handles empty violations array gracefully

requirements.text: Create a LiabilityDisplay component that visualizes the fault percentage split between parties using a progress bar or pie chart style display showing each party's liability percentage

quotes: I think we should include liability at this stay, example 70% 30%

acceptanceCriteria: Component displays liability percentages for each party
Visual representation clearly shows the percentage split (e.g., 70% / 30%)
Component uses color coding to indicate fault severity

requirements.text: Update the ReportDetail component to display the ViolationsDisplay and LiabilityDisplay components showing detected violations and calculated fault percentages
acceptanceCriteria: ReportDetail page includes ViolationsDisplay component
ReportDetail page includes LiabilityDisplay component
Both components are positioned logically within the report detail layout

constraints: Backend must remain single-actor architecture in backend/main.mo
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
GPS location data is optional - system should work without it
Violation detection must be based on photo analysis combined with GPS/timestamp when available
All liability percentages must sum to 100%
nonGoals: Real-time GPS tracking or live location services
Comprehensive UK Highway Code violation detection (only stop signs, U-turns, traffic signals)
Multi-party accident scenarios beyond two parties
Historical violation tracking or driver scoring
Manual override of calculated liability percentages
